headline: Owens Corning — $250M, 30-year HQ stay + Riverwalk easement bundled in deal
date_label: Reported Apr 7–8 · 6 weeks ago · council vote pending
paragraphs: In exchange for the property-tax incentive extension, Owens Corning will invest $250M in building and technology improvements, create 25 new jobs, maintain 1,036 existing high-wage jobs, and commit to keeping its world HQ downtown for 30 more years. New detail already in the public record: alongside the 30-year commitment, Owens Corning will enter a payment-in-lieu-of-taxes agreement with Toledo Public Schools and a separate agreement with the city and Metroparks Toledo allowing the final phase of the Glass City Riverwalk to be built on the Owens Corning campus . April reporting indicated a final City Council vote was expected at month-end; no formal vote outcome has surfaced this window.
why_it_matters: The campus easement is the under-reported detail — it means the Riverwalk's terminal phase is no longer dependent on a future negotiation; it's bundled into the same deal that gives Owens Corning its tax extension. That removes one of the largest tail risks on completing the 10-year Riverwalk build by 2032. Track the council vote outcome; if it stalls, the Riverwalk completion math gets harder.
search_terms: Owens Corning Toledo headquarters tax incentive 250 million 30 year Riverwalk easement TPS PILOT
